examples: The Montgolfier brothers decided that the balloon was now ready for passengers.
The montgolfier brothers began experimenting by filling small paper bags with smoke.
In 1782 two Frenchmen, Joseph and Etienne Montgolfier, made a balloon out of paper and cloth.
In 1783, brothers Etienne and Joseph Montgolfier made the first public demonstration of a hot-air balloon.
In 1783, brothers Etienne and Joseph Montgolfier made the first public demonstration of a hot air balloon.
